run_summary: Portfolio is up modestly across six holdings; all theses intact. BLDR appears twice (two separate buys at different times); consolidation logic deferred to execution layer. New flags present a mixed picture: ONTO is a RATIONAL conf-3 M&A signature worth a small exploratory test given the named 8-K catalyst; ALK and PLAB are textbook pre-event/sector-tailwind fades (OVERDONE conf-3) that meet SKIP criteria because the risk/reward is asymmetric against them without new confirmed information; BLDR re-appears as UNDERDONE conf-3 with a named policy catalyst (housing bill), but I already hold it twice and the sector-rotation trade is captured—adding a third position would violate prudent concentration and adds no new signal.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5/21 8-K with material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance; today's price action is semi-sector tailwind.
entries.thesis: Today's move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), but the 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01 looks like a financing/acquisition package. That combination most often signals an M&A deal funded with debt + stock issuance. Volume is actually below average today, so the catalyst is not driving incremental flow — the SMH tailwind is doing the work.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K turns out to be a routine convertible note refinancing with no strategic element, the bundle is non-event and ONTO is purely a sector trade.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: RATIONAL conf-3 on a named 8-K catalyst (5/21 multi-item filing: 1.01 material agreement + 2.03 new debt + 3.02 unregistered equity issuance). The bundle is a recognized M&A-with-financing signature, and the thesis explicitly identifies the learning signal (bundled 8-K items as high-probability M&A marker). Today's +4.55% move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%); volume is below average, so the catalyst itself is not yet visible to the tape. This is a genuine exploratory case: real catalyst URL, coherent setup, actionable 8-K bundle, but confidence only 3 and the stock is moving on sector beta not on the acquisition story itself. A 6% exploratory position tests whether the 8-K signature thesis is tradeable. If the 8-K resolves as routine refinancing, I learn the signal is weaker than expected.

red_team.weakest_link: The 8-K signature thesis assumes a specific bundle of items (1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02) reliably predicts M&A, but the filing may contain routine refinancing or unrelated corporate housekeeping that superficially matches the pattern.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on pattern recognition—that a specific 8-K item bundle is a high-probability M&A marker—but does not verify the actual content of the 5/21 filing or check whether this pattern has genuine predictive power versus coincidence. The volume is below average (0.89x) and the move is purely sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), which means the market has not yet priced in the catalyst story itself; this could indicate the bundle is indeed non-event noise. The 6% position is sized as an exploratory test of the signal, which is appropriate for confidence-3, but the thesis conflates 'the items were filed together' with 'this is acquisition financing' without intermediate evidence.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Crude oil weakness (XLE -2.76%) and ceasefire repricing; no company-specific news
entries.thesis: Airlines rally on falling oil is a textbook reflex trade, and ALK has run hard in five sessions with no 8-K, no earnings nearby, and only marginal volume confirmation today. With XLE -2.8% and ceasefire talk swirling, the move is plausibly a crude-down reflex layered on a small float. The magnitude of the multi-day run looks ahead of the actual fuel-cost benefit and exposes the stock if oil reverses on any Hormuz escalation.
entries.what_kills: Concrete sell-side capacity/PRASM upgrade or M&A chatter would justify the move on fundamentals rather than fuel reflex.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf-3 on a five-day +17.21% rally purely on oil-reflex tailwind (XLE -2.76%, ceasefire talk) with no company 8-K or earnings catalyst. The thesis is sound—pre-event rallies of this size create asymmetric downside—but the bar for acting on a SKIP-eligible flag is higher when my overall OVERDONE hit rate is 57% (not bad, but not convex upside). More importantly: no named company catalyst means the setup rests entirely on crude reversal, which is outside my direct information set. If oil rallies tomorrow on Hormuz news, ALK could fade sharply, but I have no principled way to predict crude direction beyond the macro pulse. The setup is real but unanchored to a company event I can monitor. SKIP and watch for a reversal print to short into.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semicap sector rally + pre-earnings positioning into 5/28 pre-market print
entries.thesis: PLAB reports tomorrow pre-market and has rallied notably into the print on a day when SMH is up 4.5%. Photomask is a niche corner of semicap with much less direct AI-memory exposure than the names actually driving the SK Hynix headline. Pre-earnings rallies of this magnitude in mid-cap semis create asymmetric fade setups — the bar is now elevated heading into a print where any guide miss gets punished hard, and the stock has already captured the sector tailwind plus more.
entries.what_kills: A clear beat-and-raise tied to advanced-node photomask demand acceleration would justify the run-up and continue it.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf-3 on a pre-earnings rally (+14.54% in 5 days, +5.79% today into a 5/28 pre-market print). The thesis is tight—elevated bar into earnings print, especially for a non-core-AI photomask supplier—but the execution problem is timing. I do not short earnings, and a 6% exploratory long position into a print 1 day away is not a sound trade structure. If the print gap-downs tomorrow, the entry will be bad. If it gaps up, I am chasing. The thesis is sound but the horizon is too short and the event risk is too binary to justify even an exploratory entry today. SKIP and revisit post-print if the stock rolls over.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: House passes housing affordability bill (CNBC: 'win for investors'); 8-K with 5.02 director/officer changes around annual meeting
entries.thesis: House approved housing affordability bill today (removed industry-opposed build-to-rent requirements — explicitly framed as 'win for investors'). BLDR is a direct beneficiary as the largest building products supplier. Move is on only 1.02x volume despite a meaningful policy catalyst, and the stock is still negative on a 5-day basis — suggesting the housing-bill news hasn't been fully absorbed by funds yet. The 8-K's 5.02 officer item adds some noise but appears coincident with annual meeting (5.07 alongside), likely board-rotation routine.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 item turns out to be unplanned CFO/CEO departure; Senate kills bill; housing data prints weak
entries.reasoning: UNDERDONE conf-3 on today's House housing-bill passage and on 8-K 5.02 director/officer changes around annual meeting. This is a valid catalyst and the thesis is identical to (or overlapping with) the second BLDR position I own (8 shares @ 73.9839, entered 4 days ago on the same housing-bill catalyst narrative). I already hold 17 BLDR shares (~14% of portfolio), and the Industrials sector is at ~24% of equity. Adding a third BLDR position would push Industrials above 30% sector weight (approaching the 40% hard limit) and BLDR to >20% single-name exposure (approaching the 25% position cap). The housing-bill thesis is captured in my existing two positions. A third buy would be concentration-driven, not signal-driven. SKIP and let existing positions run.

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: 5/21 earnings 8-K, continuation move on risk-on tape
entries.thesis: DECK printed earnings via 8-K on 5/21 and has continued higher into today on above-average volume. Without the actual print details (guidance, HOKA/UGG splits) I cannot tell if the continuation is justified follow-through on a beat or short-covering overshoot in a quality compounder that had been beaten down. Both bull and bear cases require the segment data I don't have.
entries.what_kills: Either side requires the actual report contents to commit.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Possibly China steel demand read-through; no filings
entries.thesis: AMR jumped on 1.5x volume with no 8-K, no earnings, in a tape where energy is selling off hard. Met coal is decoupled from thermal/crude, so the energy divergence isn't dispositive. China industrial profits +24.7% in April could be the catalyst (steel demand proxy → met coal), but the connection is speculative without confirming flow data. Neither bull nor bear case is articulable at conf 3+ without more.
entries.what_kills: If peers don't move and AMR is alone, it's likely a low-float technical squeeze without fundamental support.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No 8-K, no earnings within the window. Possibly sympathy with semi rotation or activist/M&A chatter (Starboard has historically held QRVO).
entries.thesis: Move has now compounded to a significant multi-day run without an 8-K or earnings event. Could be activist/M&A speculation (QRVO has been a recurring activist target), sympathy with broader semi rotation away from NVDA into smaller chip names (SMH strong, NVDA weak), or leaked sell-side note. Without a visible driver, direction-from-here is genuinely uncertain.
entries.what_kills: Discovery that the move is purely retail/momentum with no fundamental driver — would argue for fade. Alternatively, confirmed activist 13D would invalidate the 'no catalyst' framing entirely.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.catalyst: May 21 8-K with 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure (content unknown from filing codes alone).
entries.thesis: BMI filed a 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure on May 21 and the stock has continued higher into today. Reg-FD filings can be either bullish (investor day reaffirming/raising outlook) or neutral (boilerplate conference deck). Without knowing the content of the filing, I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+. BMI is a low-beta water-meter compounder that rarely moves this much without substance, which slightly biases bullish, but not enough to commit.
entries.what_kills: The 7.01 is routine investor conference boilerplate — in which case the move is sentiment/sympathy with the utility/water infrastructure theme and could fade.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector strength in defense names (ITA +0.99%); AVAV is high-beta within that group. No company-specific catalyst visible.
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with ITA +0.99% and ongoing defense procurement narrative (drones/loitering munitions remain a priority regardless of any Iran de-escalation — Ukraine/Pacific demand persists). Magnitude is larger than the sector ETF but AVAV is high-beta within defense and is a pure-play on the drone theme that has been a structural winner. No 8-K, no manufactured thesis warranted — the move looks proportionate to ongoing tailwinds rather than mispriced.
entries.what_kills: An Iran peace deal genuinely reduces defense spending expectations and AVAV gives back gains — would suggest the move was actually overdone and tied to the Iran narrative inverse to what I'm reading.

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown. Possibly networking sympathy or short squeeze.
entries.thesis: Volume multiple is below 1x (0.89), which is unusual for a sharp single-day move and weakens the conviction that institutional buyers are accumulating on news. Could be sympathy with networking peers (ANET, CSCO) on AI-networking theme but neither moved sharply today. No 8-K. The combination of sharp move + below-average volume + no visible catalyst is a yellow flag — could be a thin-tape squeeze rather than informed buying.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays light and the move fades over the next 1-2 sessions — classic thin-tape pop pattern.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Unidentified; SMH +3.8% tape provides partial explanation but not full magnitude
entries.thesis: LSCC up sharply on 1.4x volume with empty catalyst_signals. SMH +3.8% explains some of it but LSCC tripled that. Possible drivers: sell-side note, M&A whisper, or AI-FPGA narrative tailwind — but I cannot identify which from available evidence. Neither bull nor bear case clears conf 3.
